sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32400151 with SDR Consulting for classification and compensation services to
cover the period of February 26, 2023 through June 30, 2026, for a total cost not to
exceed $150,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the contract is budgeted in the Human Resources Department through its
contractual services allocations.
sections.current_situation:
Human Resources Analysts in the HR Department are a small cadre entrusted with
assignments spanning recruitment, exams, employee transactions, benefits, and
workers’ compensation, leaving little capacity for tasks related to classification and
compensation (“class and comp”), which entails creating new job classifications (or
updating them whenever necessary), performing salary surveys, and conducting desk
audits.
As such, the HR Department collaborates with independent consultant Shelly Del
Rosario (SDR Consulting) to expand its bandwidth and to provide these services to
client departments in an efficient and objective manner. SDR Consulting has
consistently delivered a high-caliber work product to the City.
sections.background:
As part of its reorganization over the last two years, the HR Department has engaged a
pair of independent consultants – one of them being SDR Consulting – to ensure timely
delivery of the City’s “class and comp” needs.

E-Mail: manager@CityofBerkeley.info Website: http://www.CityofBerkeley.info/Manager
Page 2 of 3
Contract Amendment: SDR Consulting CONSENT CALENDAR
December 10, 2024
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Limited staffing in the HR Department’s analyst rank and the City’s ongoing needs for
classification and compensation services necessitate the engagement of external
consultants. Extension of a contract with SDR Consulting would bolster timely
performance of classification and compensation studies that City departments need.
sections.alternatives:
None.
CONTACT PERSON
Aram Kouyoumdjian, Director of Human Resources, (510) 981-6807.
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32400151 AMENDMENT: SDR CONSULTING FOR
CLASSIFICATION AND COMPENSATION SERVICES
WHEREAS, the Human Resources Department is entrusted with the City’s classification
and compensation (“class and comp”) function;
WHEREAS, limited staffing in the HR Department requires engagement of independent
contractors to perform class and comp work – creating new job classifications (or updating
them), performing salary surveys, and conducting desk audits – in an efficient and timely
manner;
WHEREAS, independent consultant Shelly Del Rosario, providing services as SDR
Consulting, has been performing such classification and compensation work for the City
and consistently delivering high-caliber product;
WHEREAS, extension of the SDR Contract would facilitate timely delivery of class and
comp work needed by City departments.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute an amendment to Contract No. 32400151 with
SDR Consulting for an amount not to exceed $150,000 for the period covering February
23, 2023 through June 30, 2026; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that a record signature copy of said contract and any
amendments be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
